signals.reasoning: No live position and no real edge. BTC is basically flat, funding is only slightly negative, and the mechanical board is neutral. With event veto active and macro still RED, this is not the time to force a new major-position swing.

ETH remains soft but not decisively trending enough for a fresh short, and there is no squeeze or dislocation on the long side. Neutral mechanics plus active veto make patience better than low-quality action.

Of the majors, SOL is still one of the cleaner bearish setups: price is down on the day, funding is still positive, and the board leans bearish. Confidence stays moderate because this would be a new trade under RED macro and active veto.

It is still hot on price, but this is not a disciplined new long: spread is very wide, liquidity is poor, and the board is neutral rather than squeeze-driven. Under veto conditions this stays a watch-only name.

The tape is weak and the mechanical board is bearish, with price down materially and intraday action still leaning lower. This is cleaner than the meme squeezes, but volume is thin and cascade risk is high, so it only earns a moderate short bias.

TRUMP is not giving a clean new signal either way. Price is only modestly up, funding is mildly positive, and the board stays neutral. In a vetoed RED regime, that is not enough to justify new exposure.

SKR remains one of the cleaner bearish small-cap boards: price is down, longs are still paying funding, and the mechanical bias is outright bearish. Confidence is capped by weak volume and wide spread, but the direction is still cleaner than the long side.

The squeeze thesis is still alive mechanically with strongly negative funding and a large 24h gain, but the setup quality is worse with veto active: spread is very wide, liquidity is limited, and the move is no longer fresh enough to chase. I would not force a new long here.

BLUR still has a bullish squeeze profile, but with no live position to defend and the event veto back on, the bar for re-entry is higher. The tradeable continuation case exists, yet it is not strong enough to justify a fresh buy right now under RED macro and a 0.25 risk environment.
